name: django4-background-tasks

metadata.name: django4-background-tasks
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# Django 4 Background Tasks

:::{note}
This is an adaptation of django-background-tasks that removes django-compat and makes other small changes to allow compatilibity with Django 4.0.

It is also compatible with Django 3 (at least).
:::

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/django4-background-tasks/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: http://django4-background-tasks.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/django4-background-tasks.svg
:alt: PyPI
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/django4-background-tasks
```

Django Background Task is a database-backed work queue for Django, loosely based around [Ruby's DelayedJob] library. This project was adopted and adapted from [lilspikey] `django-background-task`.

To avoid conflicts on PyPI we renamed it to \[django4-background-tasks\](<https://pypi.org/project/django4-background-tasks/>). For an easy upgrade from django-background-tasks to django4-background-tasks, the internal module structure were left untouched.

In Django Background Task, all tasks are implemented as functions (or any other callable).

There are two parts to using background tasks:

- creating the task functions and registering them with the scheduler
- setup a cron task (or long running process) to execute the tasks

## Docs

See [Read the docs].

[lilspikey]: https://github.com/lilspikey/
[read the docs]: http://django4-background-tasks.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
[ruby's delayedjob]: https://github.com/tobi/delayed_job

documentation: Control how many times a task will be attempted.

documentation: Maximum possible task run time, after which tasks will be unlocked and tried again.

documentation: Maximum possible task run time, after which tasks will be unlocked and tried again.

documentation: Backoff multiplier for retries.

methods.name: background_task.settings.AppSettings.BACKGROUND_TASK_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER
documentation: Backoff multiplier for retries.

methods.name: background_task.settings.AppSettings.BACKGROUND_TASK_RUN_ASYNC
documentation: Control if tasks will run asynchronous in a ThreadPool.

methods.name: background_task.settings.AppSettings.BACKGROUND_TASK_ASYNC_THREADS
documentation: Specify number of concurrent threads.

methods.name: background_task.settings.AppSettings.BACKGROUND_TASK_PRIORITY_ORDERING
documentation:
Control the ordering of tasks in the queue.
Choose either `DESC` or `ASC`.

https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nice_(Unix)
A niceness of −20 is the highest priority and 19 is the lowest priority. The default niceness for processes is inherited from its parent process and is usually 0.

functions.name: background_task.tasks.bg_runner

Executes the function attached to task. Used to enable threads.
If a Task instance is provided, args and kwargs are ignored and retrieved from the Task itself.

documentation:
`currently_locked - currently_failed` in `find_available` assues that
tasks marked as failed are also in processing by the running PID.

methods.name: background_task.models.TaskManager.new_task
documentation:
If `remove_existing_tasks` is True, all unlocked tasks with the identical task hash will be removed.
The attributes `repeat` and `repeat_until` are not supported at the moment.

documentation: Check if the locked_by process is still running.

documentation: Check if the last_error field is empty.

methods.name: background_task.models.Task.reschedule
documentation:
Set a new time to run the task in the future, or create a CompletedTask and delete the Task
if it has reached the maximum of allowed attempts

methods.name: background_task.models.Task.create_completed_task

---

functions.name: background_task.tests.test_tasks.mocked_run_task
documentation:
We mock tasks.run_task to give other threads some time to update the database.

Otherwise we run into a locked database.

functions.name: background_task.tests.test_tasks.mocked_run_next_task
documentation:

Otherwise we run into a locked database.
